One of the MANY reasons why I refuse to watch The Academy Awards is because they STILL don’t recognize or respect stuntmen & women. It’s absolutely DISGUSTING. The training, the discipline, the choreography, the pain, the sacrifice… TOTALLY IGNORED. Pretty much every person awarded an Oscar for a main category NEEDS to thank a stuntman. The actors, the directors, the cinematographers, the editors… their jobs would be a LOT harder if it weren’t for stuntmen.

I was born in the 70s, and as an avid movie watcher, I can say that there has never been more impressive stunt work in Hollywood than there has been in the past 30 years. How many behind-the-scenes clips of action scenes are uploaded to YouTube alone? Like, WE ALL know an incredible stunt when we see the actual footage! I mean, Tom Cruise has been raising the bar with every “Mission: Impossible” movie he does! 

Seriously… 95 freaking years of movie stunts… and Hollywood REFUSES to acknowledge them. It’s absolutely INSANE. It’s SINFUL. I mean, think of how many stuntmen and women have been severely injured, or even killed whilst making a movie… and their work will NOT be recognized by the Academy.
